payload.description: since sprint 1 and 2 are done. Continue with sprint 3

goal.answer: Build the social layer: a real-time feed with infinite scroll, likes, comments, new posts, and follow/unfollow functionality.

payload.plain: Build the social layer: a real-time feed with infinite scroll, likes, comments, new posts, and follow/unfollow functionality.

criteria.answer:
CommunityPage shows a real social feed from Supabase via useInfiniteQuery (20 posts per page), replacing all hardcoded PostCards
Users can like/unlike posts with optimistic UI updates that persist in Supabase
Users can view and add comments on posts through a bottom sheet dialog
Users can create new posts via a form with caption, image upload, optional plant tag, and hashtags
Users can follow/unfollow profiles and follower/following counts update
The 'Following' tab in CommunityPage filters posts to only show followed users
All TypeScript compiles with zero errors

plain: CommunityPage shows a real social feed from Supabase via useInfiniteQuery (20 posts per page), replacing all hardcoded PostCards
Users can like/unlike posts with optimistic UI updates that persist in Supabase
Users can view and add comments on posts through a bottom sheet dialog
Users can create new posts via a form with caption, image upload, optional plant tag, and hashtags
Users can follow/unfollow profiles and follower/following counts update
The 'Following' tab in CommunityPage filters posts to only show followed users
All TypeScript compiles with zero errors

constraints.answer: Must use TanStack Query for all server state, Must use react-hook-form + zod for forms, Must use useInfiniteQuery for paginated feed with 20 posts per page, Must use optimistic updates for likes (dont wait for server), Must follow existing shadcn/ui and Tailwind patterns, Must not call Supabase directly in page components — always use query hooks

plain: Must use TanStack Query for all server state
Must use react-hook-form + zod for forms
Must use useInfiniteQuery for paginated feed with 20 posts per page
Must use optimistic updates for likes (dont wait for server)
Must follow existing shadcn/ui and Tailwind patterns
Must not call Supabase directly in page components — always use query hooks

phases.answer: Query Hooks, Feed + Likes + Comments + Posts, Follow/Unfollow + Following Feed

phaseSnapshots.name: Query Hooks
phaseSnapshots.goal: All Sprint 3 data-fetching hooks exist, are type-safe, and follow the project's query patterns.
phaseSnapshots.description: Install npm dependencies and create the foundational TanStack Query hooks for posts, comments, likes, and follows.

steps.description: Create src/queries/posts.ts with useFeedPosts (useInfiniteQuery, 20 per page), useCreatePost, useLikePost, useUnlikePost
Create src/queries/comments.ts with useComments(postId) and useAddComment
Create src/queries/follows.ts with useFollows, useFollow, useUnfollow, and useIsFollowing(followingId)
Type-check the project to ensure new hooks compile

verification.command: npx tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json 2>&1 | grep -c 'error TS' || echo '0'

phaseSnapshots.name: Feed + Likes + Comments + Posts
phaseSnapshots.goal: CommunityPage shows a real infinite-scrolling social feed with likes, comments, and post creation.
phaseSnapshots.description: Wire CommunityPage to real data, adapt PostCard, add likes, comments sheet, and new-post form.

steps.description: Wire CommunityPage to useFeedPosts with useInfiniteQuery, replacing hardcoded PostCards with real post data
Adapt PostCard to accept a Post object, wire optimistic like/unlike via useLikePost + useUnlikePost, and add a comments button that opens a comments sheet
Build a CommentsSheet component (or inline in PostCard) that displays comments via useComments and allows adding comments via useAddComment
Add a NewPostSheet to CommunityPage with react-hook-form + zod for caption, image upload, optional plant tag, and tags array

verification.command: npx tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json 2>&1 | grep -c 'error TS' || echo '0'

phaseSnapshots.name: Follow/Unfollow + Following Feed
phaseSnapshots.goal: Users can follow/unfollow profiles and see a filtered feed of only followed users.
phaseSnapshots.description: Implement follow/unfollow on profiles and wire the 'Following' feed tab to filter by followed users.

steps.description: Add follow/unfollow buttons to ProfilePage using useFollow, useUnfollow, and useIsFollowing
Wire the 'Following' tab in CommunityPage to filter feed posts by followed user IDs, using the follows table
Update follower/following counts display on ProfilePage to reflect real data

verification.command: npx tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json 2>&1 | grep -c 'error TS' || echo '0'

assumptions.answer: Supabase schema (posts, likes, comments, follows tables with RLS) is already deployed — verified via REST API in Sprint 1; Database has denormalized likes_count and comments_count on posts table; PostCard component already exists and will be adapted to accept real Post data; CommunityPage is the feed route (/community) and will be wired to real data; Offset-based pagination is acceptable for the feed (AGENTS.md mentions useInfiniteQuery with 20 posts per page); User profile rows exist from Sprint 1 (required for author_id relationships); The 'Groups' and 'Challenges' tabs in CommunityPage are out of Sprint 3 scope (future sprint)

payload.summary: Query Hooks phase complete. Created posts.ts with 5 hooks (useFeedPosts with useInfiniteQuery and following filter, usePostLikeStatus, useCreatePost, useLikePost with optimistic updates, useUnlikePost with optimistic updates). Created comments.ts with 2 hooks (useComments with author join, useAddComment). Created follows.ts with 4 hooks (useFollows returning Set, useIsFollowing, useFollow, useUnfollow). All files pass TypeScript compilation with 0 errors.

Feed + Likes + Comments + Posts phase complete. CommunityPage now shows a real infinite-scrolling feed via useFeedPosts with IntersectionObserver. PostCard adapted to accept real post data with author profiles joined. Added optimistic like/unlike via useLikePost/useUnlikePost with usePostLikeStatus. Added comments bottom Sheet in PostCard with useComments and useAddComment. Added NewPostSheet to CommunityPage with react-hook-form + zod (caption required, optional tags/plant/image), image upload to post-images bucket, and FAB trigger button. TypeScript compiles with 0 errors.

Follow/Unfollow + Following Feed phase complete. Added follow/unfollow buttons to ProfilePage using useFollow/useUnfollow/useIsFollowing, conditionally hidden when viewing own profile. Shows 'Follow'/'Following'/'Unfollow' states with loading spinner. Following tab filter was already wired via useFeedPosts hook with follows table query. Added real-time follower/following counts using useFollowerCount and useFollowingCount hooks that query Supabase count() on the follows table, replacing stale profile columns. Counts invalidate on follow/unfollow success for immediate UI updates. TypeScript compiles with 0 errors.

payload.finalSummary:
Sprint 3 — Social Feature is complete across 3 phases:

**Phase 1 — Query Hooks:**
- Created posts.ts with 5 hooks: useFeedPosts (useInfiniteQuery with 20 posts/page, supports Following filter via follows table), usePostLikeStatus, useCreatePost, useLikePost with optimistic cache updates, useUnlikePost with optimistic cache updates
- Created comments.ts with 2 hooks: useComments (with author profile join), useAddComment
- Created follows.ts with 6 hooks: useFollows (Set of followed IDs), useIsFollowing, useFollow, useUnfollow, useFollowerCount (Supabase count), useFollowingCount (Supabase count)

**Phase 2 — Feed + Likes + Comments + Posts:**
- Wired CommunityPage to useFeedPosts with IntersectionObserver infinite scroll
- Replaced hardcoded PostCards with real post data
- Adapted PostCard to accept post prop with nested profiles data
- Added optimistic like/unlike via useLikePost/useUnlikePost with usePostLikeStatus
- Added comments bottom Sheet in PostCard with useComments/useAddComment
- Added NewPostSheet to CommunityPage with react-hook-form + zod (caption required, optional tags/plant/image)
- Added FAB (+ button) to trigger new post sheet
- Image upload to post-images bucket via useUpload

**Phase 3 — Follow/Unfollow + Following Feed:**
- Added follow/unfollow buttons to ProfilePage with conditional rendering (hidden for own profile)
- Shows 'Follow'/'Following'/'Unfollow' states with loading spinner
- Following tab filter wired via useFeedPosts(activeTab === 'Following')
- Replaced stale profile column counts with real-time useFollowerCount/useFollowingCount
- Counts invalidate on follow/unfollow success for immediate updates

All TypeScript compilation passes with 0 errors.

grade.rationale: All phases completed and gate rationales cite specific grep counts and tsc output, but verification is entirely proxy-sentinels (grep and static compile checks) with no evidence the app was actually run against Supabase. The diff substantiates the claimed hooks and UI changes across CommunityPage, PostCard, and ProfilePage, but the lack of runtime or real-execution verification prevents an A.
